SnowDistribution: Drifts in sheltered locations with icy slopes where exposed to the wind.  Mostly good visibility with occasional summit cloud.  Winds 30-40 mph, progress difficult at times, very cold.
Comment: Caution required when localised unstable windslab present. Icy conditions underfoot in exposed locations.
WeatherInfluences: No precipitation forecast. Moderate to fresh South, then South-South-Westerly winds. Poorly defined freezing level, cooler overnight then 900 rising to 1200 metres, maybe higher later.
SnowStability: Moderately bonded windslab will be present in wind sheltered locations on North-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Gully tops and steep slopes under corrie rims will be worst affected. Exposed locations will be scoured and icy in places. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Dry and sunny with gale force South-Westerly, locally Westerly winds. Freezing level rising to around 1100 metres, then lowering late afternoon to 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Gale force winds were redistributing available deposits, forming localised unstable windslab in steeper wind sheltered locations on North-West through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Worst affected locations are gully tops and steep slopes around corrie rims. Exposed locations are being stripped bare, exposing old, firm snow.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Still reasonable snow cover above 800 metres. Good visibility  Winds 30 -40 mph, progress difficult and tiring, blowing us over on occasions
Comment: Snowpack generally quite firm, slip and fall hazard on slopes and in gullies.
WeatherInfluences: A cloudy day which will be generally dry although a few very light rain or sleet showers are possible. The freezing level will be around 1200 metres.
SnowStability: Below 1100 metres the snowpack will slowly thaw, above this level not much change is expected, it will remain generally firm. Stability will be generally good throughout. The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with near gale South-Westerly winds. The freezing level rose to around 1200 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The generally firm snowpack softened slightly below 1000 metres during the day. Some shallow and avoidable patches of windslab remain on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. These were further consolidating in mild and settled conditions. Stability is generally good throughout. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Limited and patchy snow in specific places. Some more extensive on N aspects.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Very Breezy on the tops
Comment: Snowpack is very firm and although softening a little it still requires winter equipment: axe and crampons for security. Potential for very consequential slips and trips.
WeatherInfluences: A dry day is expected .Freezing level around 1000m overnight then above the Summits during the day. Winds very strong  Southerly throughout the forecast period.
SnowStability: The old snowpack that is present on mostly North-West through North to Easterly aspects in mainly gullies and coire back walls and sometimes extensive fields above 800m will be well bonded and stable throughout.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A blustery day with strong Southerly winds at summit levels . Cloudy above 950m. A dry day. Freezing level above the tops.
ObservedSnowStability: The thin snow cover of Friay has pretty much disappeared with only a few localised deposits of moist and soft stable snow remaining in wind sheltered places on Northerly aspects above 800m. The old snowpack which is present mostly on North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850m is firm and stable throughout. The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Wet snow throughout. Cloudy on the higher Western summits, brighter in the East. Winds 40 - 50 mph making progress difficult.
WeatherInfluences: Very strong Southerly winds. Some light rain, mainly in the afternoon. The freezing level will remain above the summits.
SnowStability: The patchy snowpack will continue to slowly thaw and stability will remain good in all locations. Greatest snow cover will be on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry, cloudy day with gale force Southerly winds. The freezing level was above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The patchy snowpack has good stability in all locations. Greatest snow cover is on North-West through North to East aspects above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Very hard snow ice on exposed windward slopes.   Excellent visibility. Winds approx 40 - 50 mph at higher elevations slowly easing during the afternoon.
Comment: Greatest accumulations can still be found above 800 metres in wind sheltered terrain and on high plateaus. The old snowpack will remain firm and icy, thawing at lower elevations during the day.
WeatherInfluences: Remaining dry with fresh to strong South-South-Westerly winds continuing. Winds will back South in the afternoon.  The freezing level will remain around summit height.
SnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab will persist mainly on steep wind sheltered North and North-East aspects above 900 meters. Isolated instabilities will be found on other aspects. Areas most affected will be gully tops, coire rims and steep convexities. Fragile cornices will remain a hazard on West through North to East aspects. Wind exposed areas will be firm, stable and icy. At lower elevations the snow will continue to slowly consolidate. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It has been a dry and sunny day. Gale force winds from the South-West eased slightly during the afternoon with the freezing level hovering around summit height.
ObservedSnowStability: Poorly bonded windslab has developed on steep wind sheltered locations on West through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Areas most affected are coire rims, gully tops and steep convexities especially in deep accumulations. Fragile cornices are present on the same aspects. Below 900 metres, the snowpack is slowly consolidating and rapidly thawing where exposed to direct sunlight. Wind exposed areas remain firm and icy. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

SnowDistribution: Hard and icy snowpack, wind scoured and bare on some aspects.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells and sunshine in the glen. Winds feeling cold, approx 10mph to 20mph.
Comment:
Icy paths and slopes with potential for very consequential slips and trips.
Wet into the start of the new working week.
WeatherInfluences: Continuing mainly dry through the period with fresh to strong Southerly winds. Rain late in the day with the freezing level remaining above the summits.
SnowStability: The firm snowpack will continue to slowly soften, at all but the highest elevations which will remain mainly firm and frozen. Stability will remain generally good in all locations. Largest continuous snow areas will be on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Dry and generally bright with some sunshine, persistently cloudy over the highest summits. Feeling cold with moderate to fresh South-Easterly winds and the freezing level slowly rising above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: Little to no change from yesterday. The snowpack has remained mainly frozen, firm and stable. Small pockets of yesterday\'s new snow have gathered behind rocks and boulders in wind sheltered locations but amounts are not significant. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.
